retry_reason: The put operation is unconditional and always succeeds (assuming correct usage); failure indicates a bug (refcount underflow), not a transient condition.
contract_summary: Batch release wrapper: decrements the reference count of every async bfq_queue (per actuator, I/O priority, and class) in the given bfq_group via __bfq_put_async_bfqq. Called during group teardown or when async queues should be destroyed. After this call, the caller must not access the queued objects. No return value; no failure retry is possible.

check.reason: The callee __bfq_put_async_bfqq is a put function on parameter 2 (a pointer to struct bfq_queue), accessing bfqq.ref. In the caller, the arguments &bfqg->async_bfqq[i][j][k] and &bfqg->async_idle_bfqq[k] provide pointers to struct bfq_queue, so the access paths bfqg.async_bfqq.ref and bfqg.async_idle_bfqq.ref correctly trace from the function parameter bfqg to the refcount field. The type chains are consistent: bfq_group contains arrays of bfq_queue, and each bfq_queue has an int ref. No indices are needed in the path for validation purposes, and the segment counts match.
